name: nautilai
description: Starfysh's collection of Claude Code plugins — AI coding agents, skills, and commands.

plugins.name: commitcraft
plugins.description: AI git workflow toolkit: conventional commits, issue validation, PR creation, and release guidance through a single skill with six workflows.

plugins.name: relay
plugins.description: Session continuity for Claude Code: transcript-grounded handoff documents that a fresh session picks up automatically. Ships the handoff skill.

plugins.name: cc-adoption-audit
plugins.description: Audit your Claude Code setup against available features and surface what you're not using but should, plus setup gaps.

plugins.name: pr-comment-review
plugins.description: Process and address review comments on the current pull request — fetch the threads, categorize, implement agreed fixes behind approval gates, push, and reply inline.

plugins.name: cc-validate-hooks
plugins.description: Validate the local Claude Code hooks configuration in settings.json and settings.local.json (project and user) — report schema errors, invalid event names, malformed matchers, and bad hook fields, with an optional --fix.

plugins.name: cc-skill-audit
plugins.description: Audit existing Claude Code skills against Anthropic's authoring guidance — diagnose under/over-triggering, tighten descriptions, de-bloat bodies, and sweep a skills directory (including installed plugins) for issues, with a scored sweep that ranks every skill worst-to-best.

plugins.name: phi-scan
plugins.description: Scan a repo for Protected Health Information (PHI under HIPAA Safe Harbor) — SSNs, emails, phones, IPs, dates, restricted ZIPs — then AI-triage findings to filter false positives. Optionally runs Django/React OWASP grep checks when that stack is detected.

plugins.name: review-plan
plugins.description: Adversarially validate an implementation plan against the actual codebase — surface risks, breaking changes, gaps, and simplifications before you build. Uses built-in agents by default and opportunistically taps review/Codex plugins when installed.

plugins.name: pr-review-deep
plugins.description: Rigorous, evidence-based code quality review of a branch or PR — abstraction design, type/boundary contracts, and behavior-preserving structural simplification. Proposes high-leverage restructurings with cited evidence; never performs them or expands scope.

plugins.name: rbac-django
plugins.description: Audit a Django/DRF + React codebase for RBAC gaps (missing permission classes, tenant-isolation leaks, IDOR, write-side gaps), threat-model the findings into ranked attacker abuse cases, then generate prioritized remediation playbooks and GitHub issues.

plugins.name: frontend-review
plugins.description: Audit a React + Tailwind frontend for architecture and design-token health — component size, prop drilling, duplicate-primitive reuse, folder organization, and Tailwind token violations (arbitrary values, dynamic class concatenation, hardcoded colors). Auto-detects the frontend source root; findings cite file:line.

plugins.name: wireframe
plugins.description: Create low-fidelity wireframes for UI planning — ASCII layouts, wiremd interactive prototypes, or Mermaid diagrams for tickets. Use before writing components to sketch structure, flows, and states.

plugins.name: dep-review
plugins.description: Evaluate Dependabot PRs with AUTO-MERGE / MERGE / SKIP / INVESTIGATE verdicts grounded in the PR diff and codebase usage. Low-risk patch / minor-dev-dep bumps that pass CI auto-merge; other merges and closes gate behind approval.

plugins.name: github-issue-auditor
plugins.description: Audit a GitHub repo's issues for cleanup — fuzzy-matched duplicates, orphaned sub-issues, unlabeled or stale items, and inconsistent labels against the repo's own auto-detected taxonomy. Read-only by default; any mutation (close, relabel, comment) is opt-in behind an approval gate.

plugins.name: autodev
plugins.description: Bounded autonomous development loop: scripted worktree lanes, fast worker subagents, objective script-based verification, and a hard 3-failure escalation to the user.
